keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Fully complies with the Swiss FOCA drone regulations
Registration through the Swiss system (CHF 10, valid indefinitely) if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
Mandatory liability insurance for drones weighing more than 250 g (coverage of CHF 1 million)
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
At least 5 km away from any airport or airfield
A very small country — cross-border flights to Switzerland or Austria require special attention
Alpine terrain: Watch out for helicopter routes
importantNotes: Liechtenstein fully complies with Swiss FOCA regulations
Same registration system as Switzerland (CHF 10, unlimited)
Vaduz Castle is a strict no-fly zone
The country is only about 25 km long — it's easy to cross the border without meaning to
The GDPR applies (EEA member) when recording identifiable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources from FOCA/BAZL and the Swiss Dronemap before every flight. This app is a tool—not a legal source. Liechtenstein follows the Swiss FOCA/EASA regulations.
